name: English-Style Summer Ale
overallimpression: A refreshing, thirst-quenching pale ale with low to medium residual malt sweetness, low to medium hop character, and medium-low to medium bitterness. Often brewed with torrified or malted wheat for character.
aroma: Hop aroma is low to medium, expressed as floral, herbal, earthy, stone fruit, citrus or other attributes. Hop character should not be assertive and should be well balanced with malt character. Fruity esters are low to medium. Diacetyl and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Residual malt sweetness is low to medium. Malt attributes such as biscuity or low levels of caramel are present. Hop flavor is low to medium, expressed as floral, herbal, earthy, stone fruit, citrus or other attributes. Perceived bitterness is medium-low to medium.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium-low. Mild carbonation traditionally characterizes draft-cask versions, but in bottled versions, a slight increase in carbon dioxide content is acceptable.
comments: The overall impression is refreshing and thirst quenching.
characteristicingredients: Torrified or malted wheat is often used in quantities of 25 percent or less.

name: English-Style Pale Mild Ale
overallimpression: A low-alcohol, malt-focused English ale where malt flavor and aroma dominate the flavor profile with very low to low hop character and bitterness.
aroma: Malt aroma dominates the profile. Hop aroma is very low to low. Fruity esters are very low to medium-low. Diacetyl is usually absent but may be present at very low levels.
appearance: Color ranges from light amber to medium amber. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Malt flavor dominates. Hop flavor is very low to low. Perceived bitterness is very low to low.

name: Session India Pale Ale
overallimpression: A lower-alcohol version of any India Pale Ale style with assertive hop aroma and flavor and high drinkability. Balance and drinkability are key.
aroma: Hop aroma is medium to high, exhibiting a wide range of attributes. Low to medium maltiness is present. Fruity esters are low to medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from straw to copper. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures. Hop haze is allowable at any temperature.
flavor: Hop flavor is medium to high with assertive overall hop character. Low to medium maltiness is present in aroma and flavor. Perceived bitterness is medium to high.
comments: Session India Pale Ales are lower alcohol versions of any of the various American, Juicy or Hazy, British or other India Pale Ale styles. Hop aroma and flavor attributes hew to the underlying India Pale Ale style being made at lower strength. Beers exceeding 5.0% abv are not considered Session India Pale Ales.

name: Juicy or Hazy Pale Ale
overallimpression: A hazy pale ale with very high fruity, tropical, juicy hop aroma and flavor. Bitterness is soft and well-integrated; mouthfeel is silky or full.
aroma: Medium-high to very high hop aroma is present, exhibiting a very wide range of attributes, especially fruity, tropical, juicy, and many others. Low to medium-low malt aroma may be present. Medium-low to medium-high fruity esters are present and can contribute to the perception of sweetness. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to light amber. Clarity can vary widely from very low haze to very high degree of cloudiness.
flavor: Medium-high to very high hop flavor, especially fruity, tropical, juicy. Low to medium-low malt flavor may be present. Perceived bitterness is low to medium and soft; may differ significantly from measured IBU levels.
mouthfeel: Body is medium-low to medium-high. Perceived silky or full mouthfeel may contribute to overall flavor profile.
comments: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness. The term 'juicy' describes taste and aroma attributes from late, large hop additions. A juicy character is not required. Some versions may exhibit very low cloudiness.
characteristicingredients: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness.

name: American-Style Strong Pale Ale
overallimpression: A stronger and more bitter American Pale Ale with high to very high hop aroma and flavor and high bitterness.
aroma: Hop aroma is high to very high, exhibiting a wide range of attributes including floral, citrus, fruity (berry, tropical, stone fruit and other), sulfur, diesel-like, onion-garlic, catty, piney, resinous, and many others. Low caramel malt aroma is allowable. Fruity esters may be low to high. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale to copper. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures. Hop haze is allowable at any temperature.
flavor: Hop flavor is high to very high. Low level maltiness may include low caramel malt character. Perceived bitterness is high.

name: Juicy or Hazy Strong Pale Ale
overallimpression: A stronger version of Juicy or Hazy Pale Ale with very high fruity, tropical, juicy hop character. Bitterness is soft; mouthfeel is silky or full.
aroma: Medium-high to very high hop aroma is present, exhibiting a very wide range of attributes, especially fruity, tropical, juicy, and many others. Low to medium-low malt aroma may be present. Medium-low to medium-high fruity esters may be present. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to light amber. Clarity can vary widely from very low haze to very high degree of cloudiness.
flavor: Medium-high to very high hop flavor, especially fruity, tropical, juicy. Low to medium-low malt flavor may be present. Perceived bitterness is low to medium and soft.
mouthfeel: Body is medium-low to medium-high. A silky or full mouthfeel may contribute to overall flavor profile.
comments: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness. These beers can exhibit astringency and heat ('hop burn') from very high hop usage rates.
characteristicingredients: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness.

name: West Coast-Style India Pale Ale
overallimpression: A highly attenuated, assertively hopped IPA with a dry, crisp finish. Hop aroma and flavor are high to very high; bitterness is medium-high to very high but not harsh.
aroma: Hop aroma is high to very high, exhibiting a wide range of attributes including floral, piney, citrus, fruity (berry, tropical, stone fruit and other), sulfur, diesel-like, catty, resinous, and many others. Malt character is low to medium-low; caramel or roasted malt character should not be present. Fruity esters range from low to medium. DMS, acetaldehyde, and diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Chill haze or hop haze is acceptable at low levels.
flavor: Hop flavor is high to very high. Low to medium-low malt flavor; no caramel or roasted character. Perceived bitterness is medium-high to very high, but not harsh.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium. Highly attenuated.
comments: These beers are highly attenuated with an assertive hop character and a dry, crisp finish. The style has progressed from original inception to modern day examples.

name: Juicy or Hazy Imperial or Double India Pale Ale
overallimpression: A strong, hazy IPA with high to intense fruity, tropical, juicy hop character. Bitterness is soft and well-integrated; mouthfeel is silky or full.
aroma: High to intense hop aroma, exhibiting a very wide range of attributes, especially fruity, tropical, juicy, and many others. Low to high malt aroma may be present. Medium-high to high fruity esters are present. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to light amber. Clarity can vary widely from very low haze to very high degree of cloudiness.
flavor: High to intense hop flavor, especially fruity, tropical, juicy. Low to high malt flavor may be present. Perceived bitterness is low to medium and soft.
mouthfeel: Body is medium to high. A silky or full mouthfeel may contribute to overall flavor profile.
comments: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness. These beers can exhibit astringency and heat ('hop burn') from very high hop usage rates. Versions with darker malts, non-traditional yeasts, fruits, spices, or other flavorings are categorized as Experimental India Pale Ale.
characteristicingredients: Grist may include oats, wheat, or other adjuncts to promote haziness.

name: Experimental India Pale Ale
overallimpression: Cutting-edge experimental IPAs of all types including White, Red, Brown, Brut, Brett, Lager, and fruited/spiced versions, as well as wood-aged and elaborated versions of classic IPA styles.
aroma: Medium to very high hop aroma, with attributes typical of hops from any origin. Very low to medium-low malt aroma. Fruity esters are low to high. Very low to low phenolic or other attributes typical of wine, champagne or Brettanomyces yeast strains may be present. Diacetyl and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from straw to very dark, varying widely with ingredients used. May range from clear to very high degree of cloudiness.
flavor: Medium to very high hop flavor. Very low to medium-low malt flavor. Perceived bitterness is low to very high.
mouthfeel: Body is very low to medium, depending on grist and yeast choice. Mouthfeel can vary widely from light to full and from dry to silky. Carbonation can range from average to high.
comments: Recognize the cutting edge of IPA brewing around the world. Beers include any of White, Red, Brown, Brut (fermented with champagne yeasts), Brett (fermented with Brettanomyces), Lager (fermented with lager yeasts), or many other IPA or Imperial IPA types, and fruited or spiced versions. Lactose may be used to enhance body and balance.
characteristicingredients: Wide-ranging: may include lactose, fruits, spices, vegetables (other than chili), honey, wood-aging, non-traditional yeasts including champagne or Brettanomyces.

name: American-Belgo-Style Ale
overallimpression: Either non-Belgian beer types featuring Belgian yeast characters, or Belgian-style beers featuring American hop varieties. Unique combinations of American hops and Belgian yeast attributes.
aroma: Medium to very high hop aroma exhibiting American-type hop aromas not usually found in traditional Belgian styles. Fruity esters are medium to high. Belgian yeast attributes such as banana, berry, apple, coriander, spice or smoky-phenolic should be in balance with malt and hops. Diacetyl, sulfur, and attributes typical of Brettanomyces should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from gold to black. Should conform to the base beer style.
flavor: Hop flavor medium to very high with American hop attributes. Malt perception typically low; specialty or roasted malts can be very low to robust in darker versions. Perceived bitterness is medium to high, in alignment with base beer style.
mouthfeel: Body is medium-low to medium, in alignment with base beer style.
comments: These beers are unique unto themselves. Brewers may be asked to provide supplemental information about the underlying beer style upon which the entry is based.
characteristicingredients: Belgian-style yeast strains combined with American-variety hops.

name: American-Style Black Ale
overallimpression: A dark, hop-forward American ale with low to medium-low caramel and dark roasted malt and medium-high to high hop character. Astringency and burnt character should be absent.
aroma: Hop aroma is medium-high to high, with fruity, citrusy, piney, floral, herbal or other aromas derived from hops of all origins. Low to medium-low caramel malt and dark roasted malt aromas are present. Fruity esters are low to medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is very dark to black. Opaque.
flavor: Hop flavor is medium-high to high. Low to medium-low caramel malt and dark roasted malt flavors. Astringency and burnt character of roast malt should be absent. Perceived bitterness is medium-high to high.
comments: Black ales that do not meet the specifications for American-Style Black Ale may possibly be categorized as Experimental India Pale Ale.

name: American-Style Imperial Porter
overallimpression: A strong, rich, full-bodied porter with medium malt, caramel and cocoa sweetness. No roast barley or strong burnt/black malt character.
aroma: Hop aroma is low to medium-high. Medium malt, caramel, and cocoa sweetness should be present. No roast barley or strong burnt/black malt character. Fruity esters are present but not overpowering. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is black. Opaque.
flavor: Medium malt, caramel, and cocoa sweetness. Hop flavor low to medium-high. Perceived bitterness is medium-low to medium. Fruity esters complement hop character and malt-derived sweetness.

name: Smoke Porter
overallimpression: A porter exhibiting mild to assertive smoke malt aroma and flavor in balance with other aroma attributes. Can be based on any traditional porter style.
aroma: Smoke malt aroma ranges from mild to assertive, in balance with other aroma attributes. Black malt character can be present in some porters. Medium to high malt sweetness with caramel or chocolate flavor attributes is acceptable. Hop aroma is none to medium. Low to medium fruity esters are acceptable.
appearance: Color is dark brown to black. Opaque.
flavor: Smoke malt flavor mild to assertive, balanced with porter attributes. Caramel or chocolate flavor attributes acceptable. Roast barley character is absent to low depending on underlying porter style. Perceived bitterness is medium to medium-high.
comments: Brewers may be asked to provide information about the traditional style of porter as well as the wood type used as a smoke source (e.g. 'alder smoked brown porter').
characteristicingredients: Smoked malt; wood smoke source varies (e.g., alder, beechwood).

name: Double Hoppy Red Ale
overallimpression: A strong red ale with medium to medium-high caramel malt character balanced by high to very high hop bitterness and high hop aroma and flavor.
aroma: Hop aroma is high, derived from any variety of hops. Medium to medium-high caramel malt character is present. Low to medium biscuit or toasted malt character may also be present. Complex alcohol flavors may be present. Fruity esters are medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from deep amber to dark copper/reddish-brown. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Hop flavor is high and balanced with other beer attributes. Medium to medium-high caramel malt character. Perceived bitterness is high to very high.
mouthfeel: Body is medium to full. Alcohol content is medium to high.

name: Imperial Red Ale
overallimpression: A very strong red ale with high caramel malt character, prominent hop flavor and very high bitterness. Very high alcohol is a hallmark.
aroma: Hop aroma is high, derived from any variety of hops. Medium to high caramel malt character is present. Complex alcohol flavors may be present. Fruity esters are medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from deep amber to dark copper/reddish-brown. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Hop flavor is prominent and balanced with other beer attributes. Medium to high caramel malt character. Perceived bitterness is very high.
mouthfeel: Body is full. Very high alcohol is a hallmark.

name: American-Style Sour Ale
overallimpression: A refreshing, pleasantly sour ale with balanced complexity and varying intensity of natural acidity. May be made in any color and at various strengths.
aroma: Hop aroma is none to high. Low malt character; in darker versions, low roasted malt, caramel, or chocolate aroma. Moderate to intense, balanced fruity esters are present. Diacetyl, DMS, and Brettanomyces should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from pale to black. Chill haze, bacteria, and yeast-induced haze is acceptable at any temperature.
flavor: Medium-low to high natural acidity expressed as a refreshing, pleasant sourness, in harmony with other attributes. Hop flavor and bitterness none to high. The acidity is usually in the form of lactic, acetic, and other organic acids.
comments: Beers exhibiting wood-derived characters or characters of liquids previously aged in wood are categorized as Wood-Aged Sour Beer. Fruited versions are categorized elsewhere.
characteristicingredients: Lactic, acetic, and other organic acids developed via acidified malt, kettle, or post-wort fermentation with various microorganisms.

name: American-Style Fruited Sour Ale
overallimpression: A pleasantly sour ale with prominent fruit aromas and flavors ranging from subtle to intense, in harmony with refreshing acidity.
aroma: Fruit aromas, ranging from subtle to intense, should be present. Hop aroma is none to high. Low malt aroma; in darker versions, low roasted malt, caramel, or chocolate. Moderate to intense, balanced fruity esters. Diacetyl, DMS, and Brettanomyces should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from pale to black depending on underlying beer style and added fruit. Chill haze, bacteria, and yeast-induced haze is acceptable.
flavor: Medium-low to high natural acidity, refreshing and pleasant. Fruit flavor harmonious with overall profile. Hop flavor and bitterness none to high; bitterness balanced with fruit character.
comments: Coconut beers are categorized as Field Beer; chili pepper beers as Chili Beer. Fruited versions of Berliner-Style Weisse and Belgian-Style Sour Ale are categorized elsewhere. Versions with wood character are categorized as Fruited Wood-Aged Sour Beer.
characteristicingredients: Fruit or fruit extracts used in mash, kettle, primary or secondary fermentation. Lactic and other organic acids.

name: Contemporary-Style Gose
overallimpression: A modern interpretation of Gose featuring lactic sourness and potentially incorporating fruits, spices, herbs, or other ingredients beyond traditional Leipzig-Style Gose.
aroma: Malt aroma is not present to very low. Hop aroma is very low to low. Horsey, leathery, or earthy aromas from Brettanomyces may be present at low levels. Low to medium lactic acid character expressed as sharp, refreshing sourness.
appearance: Color is usually straw to medium amber and can take on the color of added fruits or ingredients. Clear to hazy.
flavor: Low to medium lactic sourness, sharp and refreshing. Malt aroma and flavor not present to very low. Hop flavor very low to low. Perceived bitterness not present to medium. Added herbs, spices, flowers, fruits, or other ingredients should be in harmony with overall flavor profile.
comments: May be brewed with malted or unmalted barley, wheat, and oats. May contain other grains. Salt and coriander may or may not be present. Contemporary Gose may be fermented with pure beer yeast strains, with yeast mixed with bacteria, or spontaneously fermented.
characteristicingredients: Malted/unmalted barley, wheat, oats. Optional salt, coriander, herbs, spices, flowers, fruits.

name: South German-Style Kristal Weizen
overallimpression: A filtered version of Hefeweizen exhibiting the classic fruity and phenolic wheat beer character without yeast in suspension. Cleaner and drier than Hefeweizen.
aroma: Phenolic character often described as clove-like or nutmeg-like, sometimes smoky or vanilla-like. Banana-like ester aroma often present. Malt sweetness very low to medium-low. Hop aroma not present to very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to amber. Clear with no chill haze. Because the beer is filtered, no yeast should be present.
flavor: Phenolic clove or nutmeg, sometimes smoky or vanilla. Banana ester flavor. Malt sweetness very low to medium-low. Hop flavor not present to very low. Perceived bitterness very low. Well attenuated and very highly carbonated.
mouthfeel: Body is medium to full. Cleaner, drier mouthfeel than counterparts served with yeast.
comments: Made with at least 50 percent malted wheat. No yeast flavor.
characteristicingredients: At least 50 percent malted wheat.

name: German-Style Leichtes Weizen
overallimpression: A light, lower-alcohol version of Hefeweizen with less pronounced phenolic and estery aromas, a lighter mouthfeel and less complex flavor profile.
aroma: Phenolic and estery aromas typical of Weissbier are present but less pronounced. Malt aroma very low to medium-low. Hop aroma not present to very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to copper-amber. If served with yeast, appearance may be very cloudy.
flavor: Less complex than Hefeweizen due to lower alcohol and less yeasty flavor. Malt very low to medium-low. Hop flavor not present to very low. Perceived bitterness very low.
mouthfeel: Body is low with a lighter mouthfeel than Hefeweizen.
comments: The German word 'leicht' means light. These beers are light versions of Hefeweizen.
characteristicingredients: At least 50 percent wheat malt.

name: South German-Style Bernsteinfarbenes Weizen
overallimpression: An amber-colored wheat beer with distinct sweet maltiness and caramel or bread-like character from medium-colored malts, with less pronounced wheat beer fermentation character.
aroma: Distinct sweet maltiness and caramel or bread-like character. Phenolic and estery aromas typical of Weissbier are present but less pronounced. Hop aroma not present. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is amber to light brown. The German word 'Bernsteinfarben' means amber colored. If served with yeast, appearance may be very cloudy.
flavor: Caramel and bread-like character from medium-colored malts. Phenolic and estery character present but less pronounced. Hop flavor not present. Perceived bitterness is low. Well attenuated and very highly carbonated.
comments: Often roused during pouring. When yeast is present, will have a yeasty flavor and a fuller mouthfeel.
characteristicingredients: At least 50 percent wheat malt with medium-colored malts.

name: German-Style Rye Ale
overallimpression: A wheat-beer-style ale featuring at least 30% rye malt, with banana and clove-like esters and phenolics from wheat beer yeast.
aroma: Low to medium banana-like or other fruity ester aromas. Clove-like or other phenolic aromas should also be present. Hop aroma not present. In darker versions, malt aromas can include low-level cocoa-chocolate, caramel, toffee, or biscuit attributes.
appearance: Color is pale to very dark, with darker versions ranging from dark amber to dark brown. Chill haze is acceptable in versions packaged and served without yeast. Versions served with yeast may range from hazy to very cloudy.
flavor: Banana ester and clove-like phenolic flavors. Low to medium malt sweetness. Low-level roast malt astringency acceptable in darker versions when balanced with malt sweetness. Hop flavor not present. Perceived bitterness very low to low.
comments: Versions with yeast are often roused during pouring. When yeast is present, the beer should have a yeasty flavor and a fuller mouthfeel.
characteristicingredients: At least 30 percent rye malt; remainder typically barley and possibly wheat. Wheat beer yeast.

name: Bamberg-Style Weiss Rauchbier
overallimpression: A smoked wheat beer combining classic Weissbier character with smoky malt aroma and flavor.
aroma: Smoky malt aroma ranging from low to high. Smoke should be smooth, not harshly phenolic. Phenolic characteristics often described as clove, nutmeg, vanilla, and smoke. Banana esters often present at low to medium-high levels. In darker versions, a detectable degree of roast malt may be present without being aggressive. Hop aroma not present. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale to chestnut brown. If served with yeast, appearance may be very cloudy.
flavor: Smoky malt flavor low to high, smooth without harshness. Phenolic clove, nutmeg, vanilla. Banana esters. Hop flavor not present. Perceived bitterness is low. Well attenuated and very highly carbonated.
comments: Made with at least 50 percent wheat malt. Often roused during pouring.
characteristicingredients: At least 50 percent wheat malt; smoked malt typically from beechwood.

name: Belgian-Style Table Beer
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: A low-alcohol everyday beer with mild malt character, very low bitterness, and light body. Designed for daily consumption.
aroma: Mild malt character may be present. Hop aroma not present to very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from gold to black. Caramel color is sometimes added. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Mild malt. Hop flavor not present to very low. Perceived bitterness very low to low. Traditional versions do not use artificial sweeteners. Modern versions may incorporate post-fermentation sweeteners.
mouthfeel: Body is low. Low carbonation and aftertaste are typical.
comments: Mouthfeel is light to moderate, sometimes boosted with unfermented sugars/malt sugars.
characteristicingredients: Malted barley, wheat, rye; unmalted wheat, rye, oats, corn. Coriander or orange/lemon peel sometimes added in barely perceptible amounts.

name: Belgian-Style Session Ale
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: A modest-alcohol Belgian-style ale (2.1-5% abv) recognizing the uniqueness of Belgian brewing traditions but not conforming to specific defined Belgian style categories. Balance is key.
aroma: Very low to low malt aroma exhibiting a wide range of malt-derived attributes. Very low to low hop aroma. Phenolic spiciness may be absent or present at low levels. Fruity-ester complexity may range from low to medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color may vary widely. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Very low to low malt flavor. Very low to low hop flavor. Perceived bitterness very low to low but sufficient to balance other attributes.
mouthfeel: Body is very low to low.
comments: These beers can be lower gravity formulations of their own, or can be produced from second run wort from higher gravity beers. Wood-aged or fruited versions will exhibit attributes of wood-aging or fruit in harmony with overall flavor profile.

name: Belgian-Style Speciale Belge
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: A Belgian amber ale featuring caramel or lightly toasted malt character integrated with a fruity ester fermentation profile and low to medium-low yeast phenolics.
aroma: Malt aroma with caramel or lightly toasted malt attributes integrated with fermentation profile. Hop aroma very low to medium; noble-type hops commonly used. Medium fruity esters typical. Yeast-derived phenolic spicy flavors and aromas should be present at low to medium-low levels. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is gold to amber. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Caramel or lightly toasted malt character integrated with fermentation profile. Hop flavor very low to medium. Perceived bitterness is low to medium.
characteristicingredients: Noble-type hops commonly used.

name: Belgian-Style Quadrupel
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: A very strong dark Belgian ale with intense caramel, dark sugar and malty sweet flavors complementing complex fruit character. Strong alcohol presence balanced by other elements; well-suited for sipping.
aroma: Caramel, dark sugar and malty sweet aromas can be intense but not cloying. Complex fruity attributes reminiscent of raisins, dates, figs, grapes, or plums. Hop aroma not present to very low. Clove-like phenolic aroma may be present at low to medium-low levels. Diacetyl and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from amber to dark brown. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Intense caramel, dark sugar, malt sweetness complemented by complex fruity attributes. Hop flavor not present to very low. Perceived bitterness is low to medium. Strong perception of alcohol balanced by other flavors. Fusel alcohols should remain in balance.
mouthfeel: Body is full with creamy mouthfeel. Head should be dense and mousse-like.
comments: Well attenuated and characterized by an intense alcohol presence balanced by other flavors. Well balanced with savoring/sipping-type drinkability.

name: Specialty Saison
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: Saisons deviating from Classic Saison through use of dark malts, fruits, spices, special yeasts, wood-aging, or other ingredients. Esters, alcohol, and special ingredients should harmonize.
aroma: Medium to high fruity esters. Hop aroma low to medium-high. Malt aroma typically low to medium-low. Complex alcohols, herbs, spices, low Brettanomyces attributes (slightly acidic, fruity, horsey, goaty, leather-like), and clove-like/smoky phenolics may be present. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from straw to dark brown; may take on hue of fruit, darker malts, or other ingredients. Chill haze or slight yeast haze is acceptable.
flavor: Hop flavor low to medium-high. Malt flavor varies with specialty malts. Perceived bitterness medium to medium-high. Herb or spice flavors may be present. Low level sour acidic flavor acceptable when in balance.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium. Often bottle conditioned with high carbonation.
comments: Saisons brewed with dark malts, fruits, spices, or wide range of ingredients. Versions exhibiting wood-aging attributes are categorized as Specialty Saison.
characteristicingredients: Specialty malts, fruits, spices, herbs, flowers, vegetables, fermentable sugars, special yeasts, optionally wood-aging.

name: Contemporary Belgian-Style Spontaneous Fermented Ale
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: A modern spontaneously fermented Belgian-style ale inspired by Traditional Gueuze but incorporating specialty malts, spices, fruits, or other ingredients that produce hue and flavor outcomes differing significantly from traditional examples.
aroma: Hop aroma not present to low. Sweet malt character not present; some versions may exhibit attributes typical of specialty malts. Intense fruity esters, sour, and acidic attributes from spontaneous fermentation. Horsey, goaty, leathery, and phenolic aromas from Brettanomyces often present at moderate levels. Vanillin and other wood-derived flavors may range from absent to up to low-medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from gold to very dark. Cloudiness is acceptable, as these beers are frequently bottle conditioned.
flavor: Intense fruity ester, sour, and acidic attributes. Sweet malt character not present. Hop flavor not present to low. Perceived bitterness very low. May be very dry or mildly sweet. Brettanomyces character moderate. Carbonation can be none (flat) to high.
mouthfeel: Body is very low with dry mouthfeel.
comments: Takes inspiration from Traditional Gueuze but incorporates specialty malts, spices, fruits, or other diverse ingredients and processes.
characteristicingredients: Specialty malts, sweeteners, fruits, spices, or other ingredients beyond traditional Gueuze.

name: Other Belgian-Style Ale
category: Belgian and French Origin Ale Styles
overallimpression: Belgian-style ales recognizing the uniqueness and traditions of Belgian brewing but not fitting other defined Belgian style categories. Balance is key.
aroma: Malt perception may vary widely. Hop aroma may vary widely. Phenolic spiciness may be absent or present at low levels. Fruity-ester complexity may range from low to medium. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color may vary widely. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Malt and hop flavor may vary widely. Perceived bitterness may vary widely.
comments: Wood- and barrel-aged versions exhibiting sensory attributes of wood-aging are categorized here. Fruited versions are categorized as Belgian-style fruit beers.

name: Adambier
overallimpression: A strong, dark, hoppy ale of Dortmund origin that may or may not be sour and may be extensively aged in wooden barrels. Traditional versions may have a low or medium-low degree of smokiness.
aroma: Toast and caramel malt aroma may be present. Astringency from highly roasted malt should not be present. Hop aroma low, with attributes typical of traditional non-hybrid European hop varieties. Aging in barrels may contribute some level of Brettanomyces and lactic character.
appearance: Color ranges from light brown to very dark. Beer color may be too dark to perceive clarity. When clarity is perceivable, chill haze is absent.
flavor: Toast and caramel malt. Hop flavor low. Perceived bitterness is low to medium. Extensive aging and acidification can mask malt and hop character to varying degrees.
comments: Originated in Dortmund. Adambier may or may not use wheat. Smoke character may be absent in contemporary versions. Fruited versions with wood-aging are categorized as fruited Wood- and Barrel-Aged Sour Beers; without wood-aging, as Fruit Wheat Beers.
characteristicingredients: May or may not use wheat. Traditional non-hybrid European hops. Optional wooden barrel aging.

name: Dutch-Style Kuit, Kuyt or Koyt
overallimpression: A medieval Dutch grain-bill-driven beer featuring at least 45% oat malt and at least 20% wheat malt, with grainy or grainy-bready malt character and traditional European hopping.
aroma: Grainy or grainy-bready aroma. Hop aroma very low to low from noble hops or other traditional European varieties. Esters may be present at low levels. Diacetyl usually absent but may be present at very low levels. Acidity and sweet corn-like DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is gold to copper. Chill haze and other haze is acceptable.
flavor: Distinctive grainy or grainy-bready flavor from oat malt and wheat malt. Hop flavor very low to low. Perceived bitterness medium-low to medium.
comments: This style of beer was popular in the Netherlands from 1400-1550.
characteristicingredients: At least 45 percent oat malt and at least 20 percent wheat malt. Pale malt makes up the remainder. Noble hops or other traditional European varieties.

name: International-Style Pale Ale
overallimpression: A well-integrated, easy-drinking, refreshing pale ale exhibiting diverse hop aroma and flavor attributes from hops of various origins.
aroma: Very low to medium malt flavor and aroma. Low caramel malt aroma may be present. Hop aroma low to high. Fruity esters low to high. Diacetyl usually absent but may be present at very low levels. DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is gold to copper. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Hop flavor very low to high with diverse hop aroma and flavor attributes including floral, herbal, fruity, tropical, pine, or many others. Perceived bitterness is medium to high.
comments: Pale Ales from around the world may vary considerably from other defined pale ale styles.

name: Classic Australian-Style Pale Ale
overallimpression: A mild, easy-drinking Australian pale ale defined by perceivable fruity esters balanced by low to medium hop aroma and a dry finish.
aroma: Low malt sweetness. Hop aroma low to medium. Perceivable fruity esters present (defining character). Clean yeasty, bready character may be present. Diacetyl usually absent but may be present at very low levels. DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to copper. Chill or yeast haze is acceptable at low levels.
flavor: Low malt. Hop flavor low to medium. Perceived bitterness low to medium. Overall flavor impression is mild. Yeast in suspension if present may impact perception of bitterness.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium with a dry finish.

name: New Zealand-Style Pale Ale
overallimpression: A well-integrated, refreshing pale ale showcasing distinctive fruity hop aromas and flavors characteristic of New Zealand hop varieties.
aroma: Hop aroma medium to medium-high, exhibiting attributes including tropical fruit, passionfruit, stone fruit, cut grass, or diesel. Malt aroma very low to medium. Low to medium fruity esters acceptable but not essential. Diacetyl absent. DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to medium amber. Any of yeast, chill, or hop haze may be present at low levels but are not essential.
flavor: Hop flavor medium to medium-high with tropical fruit, passionfruit, stone fruit, cut grass, or diesel attributes. Malt flavor very low to medium. Perceived bitterness low to medium-high.
mouthfeel: Body is medium-low to medium with a dry finish.
comments: Overall impression is a well-integrated easy drinking, refreshing pale ale with distinctive fruity hop aromas and flavors.
characteristicingredients: New Zealand hop varieties.

name: New Zealand-Style India Pale Ale
overallimpression: An IPA showcasing distinctive hop attributes of New Zealand hop varieties including tropical fruit, stone fruit, sulfur/diesel, citrus, and grassy character.
aroma: High to intense hop aroma exhibiting attributes such as floral, fruity (tropical, stone fruit, and other), sulfur/diesel-like, citrusy, and grassy. Low to medium malt aroma. Fruity esters low to high, acceptable but not essential. Diacetyl and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to copper. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures. Hop haze is allowable at any temperature.
flavor: High to intense hop flavor with New Zealand hop attributes. Low to medium malt flavor. Perceived bitterness is medium-high to very high.
mouthfeel: Body is medium-low to medium with a dry finish.
comments: Hop attributes are dominant and balanced with malt character.
characteristicingredients: New Zealand hop varieties. The use of water with high mineral content may result in a crisp, dry beer.

name: Swedish-Style Gotlandsdricke
overallimpression: A traditional Swedish farmhouse ale characterized by juniper and birchwood smoked malt. Fermented with bread/bakers' yeast for unique character.
aroma: Medium-low to medium malt aroma. Birchwood smoke character from the malting process. Juniper aroma should be present. Fruity ester and yeasty aromas are medium to high. Hop aroma not present to very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale to copper. Chill haze or yeast haze is acceptable.
flavor: Juniper and birchwood smoke. Malt flavor medium-low to medium. Hop flavor not present to very low. Perceived bitterness very low to medium-low. Bread/bakers' yeast contributes unique character.
comments: Juniper aroma and flavor due to use of juniper boughs/branches and berries in the brewing process.
characteristicingredients: Birchwood smoked malt, juniper boughs/branches and berries, bread/bakers' yeast.

name: Breslau-Style Schoeps
overallimpression: A pronounced wheat-malt-driven ale (up to 80% wheat) brewed with ale yeast rather than wheat beer yeast. Available in pale or dark versions.
aroma: Pronounced malt character. Paler versions may have bready, aromatic biscuit malt attributes. Darker versions may show low roast malt bitterness, toasted or nutty malt attributes. Caramel-like malt not present. Hop aroma very low. Fruity esters may be present. Diacetyl and phenolic aromas and flavors should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to black. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures. Hue may be too dark to perceive clarity in some versions.
flavor: Pronounced malt character. Medium to medium-high malt sweetness. Hop flavor very low. Perceived bitterness medium-low to medium. Fruity esters may be present.
comments: Traditional German wheat beer yeast is not used in this style.
characteristicingredients: High proportion of pale or dark wheat malt (as much as 80%), with Pilsener and other pale, toasted, or dark specialty malts. Ale yeast (not wheat beer yeast).

name: Italian-Style Pilsener
overallimpression: A dry-hopped pilsener with assertive, crisp, aromatic hop character from late hopping and dry hopping with noble-type hops. Crisp and well-attenuated.
aroma: Pronounced and aromatic hop aroma from late hopping and dry hopping with noble-type hops. Floral, herbal, peppery, or other attributes may be present. Low malty sweet aroma. Light biscuity attributes may be present. Fruity-ester and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present. Head should be dense, pure white, and persistent.
flavor: Pronounced hop flavor from late hopping and dry hopping. Low malty sweetness. Light biscuity attributes may be present. Perceived bitterness is medium to high. Well attenuated.
comments: Hop character is assertive, crisp and aromatic.
characteristicingredients: Noble-type hops used in late hopping and dry hopping.

name: Franconian-Style Rotbier
overallimpression: An amber to dark red Franconian lager with lightly toasted and/or caramel malt character, low to medium-low noble hop character, and a clean finish.
aroma: Malty aroma with light malt sweetness and lightly toasted and/or caramel malt character. Hop aroma low to medium-low, with attributes typical of noble-type hops. DMS, diacetyl, fruity esters, and phenolic attributes should not be present.
appearance: Color ranges from amber to dark red. Clear to slightly hazy for unfiltered versions. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Lightly toasted and/or caramel malt character. Hop flavor low to medium-low. Perceived bitterness low to medium-low, producing a clean finish.
characteristicingredients: Noble-type hops.

name: Bamberg-Style Helles Rauchbier
overallimpression: A pale smoked lager featuring prominent malt character and beechwood smoke ranging from very low to medium, with no harsh phenolics.
aroma: Prominent malt character with lightly toasted sweet malted barley. Beechwood smoke very low to medium; not harshly phenolic. Sulfur may be present at low levels. No caramel character. Hop aroma not present to low, from noble-type hops. Fruity esters and diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is light pale to gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Lightly toasted malt sweetness with beechwood smoke flavor. Smoke flavor may create a perception of mild sweetness. Hop flavor very low to low. Perceived bitterness is medium.
comments: Very low levels of sulfur-related compounds are acceptable.
characteristicingredients: Beechwood smoked malt; noble-type hops.

name: Bamberg-Style Bock Rauchbier
overallimpression: A strong, dark smoked bock with medium to medium-high malt character and very low to medium-high beechwood smoke. Smooth smoke flavor with no harsh phenolics.
aroma: Medium to medium-high malt aroma with very low to medium-high beechwood smoke. Smoke flavor smooth without harshness. Smoke flavor may create a perception of mild sweetness. Hop aroma very low. Fruity esters usually absent or very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is dark brown to very dark. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Medium to medium-high malt character with smooth beechwood smoke. Hop flavor very low. Perceived bitterness is medium, increasing proportionately with starting gravity.
characteristicingredients: Beechwood smoked malt.

name: Contemporary American-Style Lager
overallimpression: An updated American lager with increased hop aroma and flavor compared to traditional versions. Clean, crisp, and aggressively carbonated.
aroma: Malt sweetness and aroma very low to low. Hop aroma very low to low. Fruity esters usually absent but may be present at very low levels. Diacetyl, acetaldehyde, and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Malt sweetness very low to low. Hop flavor very low to low. Perceived bitterness very low to low.
comments: Corn, rice, or other grain or sugar adjuncts are often used, but all-malt formulations are also made. Typically exhibit increased hop aroma and flavor compared to traditional versions.
characteristicingredients: May include corn, rice, or other grain/sugar adjuncts; all-malt formulations also used.

name: Contemporary American-Style Light Lager
overallimpression: A light-bodied, reduced-calorie lager with more hop character than traditional American Light Lager. Highly attenuated and aggressively carbonated.
aroma: Malt sweetness very low but present. Hop aroma low to medium-low with wide range of hop profiles possible. Fruity esters usually absent but may be present at very low levels. Diacetyl, acetaldehyde, and DMS should not be present.
appearance: Color is very light to medium amber. The word 'Light' refers to light body and reduced calories rather than color. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Malt very low but present. Hop flavor low to medium-low and more evident than in traditional American-Style Light Lager. Perceived bitterness very low to low. Highly attenuated; final gravity often less than 1.000.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium-low, often with dry mouthfeel. High carbonation.
comments: Calories should not exceed 125 per 12-ounce serving. Low carb beers should have a maximum carbohydrate level of 3.0 gm per 12 oz.
characteristicingredients: May include corn, rice, or other grain/sugar adjuncts; all-malt formulations also used.

name: Contemporary American-Style Pilsener
overallimpression: A modernized American Pilsener diverging from pre-Prohibition versions through use of a wide range of hop aroma and flavor attributes.
aroma: Malt aroma medium-low to medium. Hop aroma medium to high with attributes typical of a wide range of hop varieties. Fruity esters, DMS, acetaldehyde, and diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Malt medium-low to medium. Hop flavor medium to high. Perceived bitterness is medium to medium-high.
comments: All-malt grists are commonly used; up to 25% corn or rice may be incorporated. Diverges from American-style pre-Prohibition lagers by virtue of a wide range of hop aroma and flavor attributes.
characteristicingredients: All-malt grists or up to 25% corn or rice; wide range of hop varieties.

name: American-Style India Pale Lager
overallimpression: A clean, crisp lager featuring fresh hop character with medium to high hop aroma and flavor and assertive bitterness.
aroma: Hop aroma medium to high with attributes typical of hops of any origin. Malt aroma medium-low to medium, exhibiting bready, cracker-like, or other attributes typical of pale malts. Fruity esters range from absent to very low. DMS, acetaldehyde, and diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Hop haze is allowable. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Hop flavor medium to high. Malt flavor medium-low to medium with bready, cracker-like attributes. Perceived bitterness is medium to high, but not harsh.
comments: Should exhibit the fresh character of hops. Some versions may be brewed with corn, rice, or other adjuncts.
characteristicingredients: Pale malts; hops of any origin. May include corn, rice, or other adjuncts.

name: American-Style Malt Liquor
overallimpression: A higher-strength American lager with some malt sweetness, low bitterness, and varied character. Some malt liquors are only slightly stronger than American lagers, while others approach bock strength.
aroma: Some malt sweetness present. Hop aroma not present. Fruity esters and complex alcohol aromas acceptable at low levels. Alcohol should not be solvent-like. DMS, diacetyl, and acetaldehyde should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Some malt sweetness. Hop flavor not present. Perceived bitterness is very low. Complex alcohol flavors acceptable at low levels.
comments: Beers of this style are varied in character.

name: American-Style Maerzen/Oktoberfest
overallimpression: An American version of the classic German Maerzen/Oktoberfest distinguished by a more pronounced hop character. Light toasted malt with low caramel and herbal, grassy, spicy, floral, or citrus hop attributes.
aroma: Sweet maltiness with light toasted character. Bready or biscuity malt aroma is acceptable. Low level caramel attributes acceptable. Hop aroma low to medium-low exhibiting herbal, grass-like, spicy, floral, or citrus attributes. Fruity esters and diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale to reddish brown. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Light toasted malt. Bready or biscuity. Low caramel acceptable. Hop flavor low to medium-low with herbal, grassy, spicy, floral, or citrus attributes. Perceived bitterness medium-low to medium.
comments: This American version of a classic German beer is distinguished by a more pronounced hop character.

name: Mexican-Style Light Lager
overallimpression: A pale, dry, light-bodied lager with low malt and hop character, made with corn and/or rice to lighten body. Highly carbonated and refreshing.
aroma: Low to medium-low pale malt sweetness and aroma. May have adjunct flavors from corn and/or rice. Hop aroma very low to low with attributes typical of noble-type hops. Low fruity esters may be present; sulfur may be present in very low levels. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is straw to pale gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Low to medium-low pale malt. Adjunct flavors from corn/rice possible. Hop flavor very low to low. Perceived bitterness very low to low.
mouthfeel: Very low to low body with dry finish. Medium to medium-high carbonation.
comments: Pale lagers often made with 2-row pilsener malt; may contain corn and/or rice to lighten body.
characteristicingredients: 2-row pilsener malt; often corn and/or rice; noble-type hops.

name: Mexican-Style Pale Lager
overallimpression: A pale, dry lager with subtle malt and noble hop character, lightened with corn and/or rice. Slightly more noble hop character than its light counterpart.
aroma: Low to medium-low pale malt sweetness and aroma. May have adjunct flavors from corn and/or rice. Hop aroma low with attributes typical of noble-type hops. Low fruity esters may be present; sulfur may be present in very low levels. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale to deep gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Low to medium-low pale malt. Adjunct flavors possible. Hop flavor low. Perceived bitterness low to medium-low.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium-low with dry finish. Head retention may be medium-low to medium.
characteristicingredients: 2-row pilsener malt; often corn and/or rice; noble-type hops.

name: Mexican-Style Amber Lager
overallimpression: An amber lager with caramel and bready malt character, light corn/rice notes, and very low hop character. Refreshing and dry-finishing.
aroma: Medium-low to medium-high caramel and bready malt aroma. Light roast and light corn and/or rice flavors may be present. Hop aroma none to low with attributes typical of noble-type hops. Low fruity esters may be present; sulfur may be present at very low levels. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is light amber to copper. Appearance should be clear with white head of foam. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Caramel and bready malt. Light roast and light corn/rice possible. Hop flavor none to low. Perceived bitterness very low to low.
mouthfeel: Light to medium-light body with light creaminess. Medium to medium-high carbonation with dry finish.
comments: Addition of specialty malts may provide caramel or lightly toasted complexity. Presence of corn/rice to lighten body may be noticeable.
characteristicingredients: Specialty malts for caramel or toasted complexity; often corn and/or rice; noble-type hops.

name: Mexican-Style Dark Lager
overallimpression: A complex dark lager with bready, toasty malt aroma and sweetness, light caramel/toast/nutty/toffee flavors, and a dry finish.
aroma: Medium to medium-high complex bready, toasty malt aroma and sweetness from darker specialty malts. Light caramel, toast, nutty, and/or toffee flavors may be present. Low aromas and flavors from corn and/or rice may be present. Hop aroma none to low with noble-type hops. Low fruity esters may be present; sulfur very low. Diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is dark copper to dark brown with ruby or garnet highlights. Appearance should be clear with creamy beige to light brown foam. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Complex bready, toasty malt with light caramel, toast, nutty, toffee flavors. Corn/rice possible. Hop flavor none to low. Perceived bitterness very low to low.
mouthfeel: Body is medium to medium-high with dry finish.
comments: Grain bill may contain Munich and/or Vienna type base malts. May also contain corn and/or rice. Small amounts of toasted and crystal/dextrin malts may be added for color and slight sweetness.
characteristicingredients: Munich and/or Vienna base malts; may include corn and/or rice; small amounts of toasted and crystal/dextrin malts.

name: West Coast-Style Pilsener
overallimpression: A balanced fusion showcasing fresh, assertive Pacific Northwest or Southern Hemisphere hop expression with the crisp drinkability of a pilsener. Significantly dry hopped.
aroma: Hop aroma medium-high to very high, exhibiting a wide range of attributes including floral, piney, citrus, fruity (berry, tropical, stone fruit, and other), sulfur, dank, diesel-like, catty, resinous, and many others. Malt aroma low to medium with pilsener malt attributes such as bready, toasty, or cracker-like. Fruity esters range from absent to medium-low. DMS, acetaldehyde, and diacetyl should not be present. Light sulfur character that enhances hop expression is allowable.
appearance: Color is straw to gold. Hop haze or chill haze is allowable at low levels.
flavor: Pronounced hop flavor showcasing modern IPA hops. Malt low to medium with pilsener malt attributes. Perceived bitterness is medium to high.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium. In balance to support high dry hop rates.
comments: Crispness and drinkability are at the forefront, but body and structure are needed to balance a significant dry hop load. Beers dry hopped with European/noble hops should be classified as Italian-Style Pilsener.
characteristicingredients: Kettle hops can be European/noble or modern IPA hops; dry hopping showcases modern IPA hops.

name: Rice Lager
overallimpression: A lager brewed with 15% or more rice. Rice character may range from clean and neutral to expressive with nutty, savory, or aromatic notes. Clean fermentation profile with dry finish.
aroma: Subtle malty sweet aroma at low levels. Very mild fusel or ester-derived sweetness from rice may be present. Rice character may range from clean/neutral to expressive (nutty, savory, aromatic). Hop aroma low to medium and in harmony with malt. Very low fruity esters may be present. Acetaldehyde, DMS or diacetyl should not be present.
appearance: Color is pale straw to gold. Appearance should be clear. Chill haze should not be present.
flavor: Subtle malt sweetness. Rice character ranges from neutral to expressive. Hop flavor low to medium. Perceived bitterness low to medium. Hops should be integrated, not pronounced.
comments: Beers featuring a dominant hop or dry hop profile are classified into other hoppy lager categories.
characteristicingredients: 15% or more rice (variety-dependent character).

name: Session Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Any style of beer at or below 5.0% abv with original gravity and alcohol content below the range of the classic style. Balance and drinkability are key.
aroma: Malt attributes should mimic the classic style upon which the beer is based, but at lower overall intensity due to lower original gravity. Hop attributes should mimic the classic style upon which the beer is based, but at lower overall intensity.
appearance: The color should mimic the classic style upon which the beer is based. Appearance may vary from brilliant to hazy to cloudy and should mimic the classic style upon which the beer is based.
flavor: Malt and hop flavor should mimic the classic style at lower overall intensity. Perceived bitterness should mimic the classic style at lower overall intensity in order to maintain the balance typical of that style.
comments: Includes beers of any style that are at or below 5.0% abv and have OG/alcohol below the range of the classic style. A beer at or below 5.0% abv that adheres to specifications of a classic style is not categorized as Session Beer. A beer below 0.5% abv is categorized as Non-Alcohol Malt Beverage.
characteristicingredients: Varies with underlying style.

name: Chocolate or Cocoa Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Any beer of any classic style incorporating dark chocolate or cocoa. Chocolate or cocoa attributes should be apparent, ranging from subtle to intense, in harmony with the overall flavor profile.
aroma: Medium-low to medium-high malt sweetness balanced with cocoa flavors and aromas. Hop aroma may vary based on underlying style and often may be lower than designated for underlying style. Fermentation typical of underlying beer style. Chocolate/cocoa attributes apparent.
appearance: Color can range from pale to very dark depending on the underlying style. Clear to hazy is acceptable.
flavor: Malt sweetness balanced with cocoa/chocolate flavors. Hop flavor varies with underlying style. Perceived bitterness very low to medium-low.
comments: Beers made with white chocolate do not typify this category; however, beers clearly exhibiting white chocolate attributes could be categorized here.
characteristicingredients: Dark chocolate or cocoa in any form.

name: Dessert Stout or Pastry Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: A strong, sweet, full-bodied beer built on a strong dark beer base, incorporating culinary ingredients to create rich flavor profiles mimicking desserts, pastries, or candies.
aroma: Extremely rich malty aroma. Coffee, caramel, roasted malt, or chocolate aromas may be evident. Hop aroma very low if present. Fruity esters may be present at low levels. Diacetyl, if present, should be at low levels. High alcohol content is evident.
appearance: Color is deep amber to black. Beer color may be too dark to perceive. Chill haze acceptable at low temperatures.
flavor: Extremely rich malty flavor with coffee, caramel, roasted malt, or chocolate. Culinary ingredients (chocolate, coffee, coconut, vanilla, maple syrup, peanut butter, marshmallow, fruits, nuts, spices) create dessert-like flavors. Hop flavor very low if present. Perceived bitterness not present to low. Addition of sugars may contribute to pronounced sweetness.
comments: The combination of a dark beer base, elevated alcohol content, and rich, sweet, dessert-like flavor profiles sets this style apart. Wood- or barrel-aged versions are classified elsewhere.
characteristicingredients: Chocolate, coffee, coconut, vanilla, maple syrup, peanut butter, marshmallow, fruits, nuts, spices, and added sugars.

name: Coffee Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: A beer of any style incorporating coffee in any form. Coffee character is the defining attribute, ranging from subtle to intense and harmonious with the underlying beer.
aroma: Coffee character apparent, ranging from subtle to intense. Malt and hop aroma vary with underlying style. Other flavors from flavored coffee may also be present. Fermentation typical of underlying style.
appearance: Color is pale to black depending on the underlying style. Clear to hazy is acceptable.
flavor: Coffee defining attribute, in harmony with underlying beer. Malt and hop flavor vary with underlying style. Perceived bitterness varies with underlying style.
mouthfeel: Body reflective of underlying beer style.
comments: Coffee character should be apparent as the defining attribute.
characteristicingredients: Coffee in any form (whole bean, ground, cold brew, espresso, flavored, etc.).

name: Specialty Honey Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Any beer incorporating honey as a fermentable sugar in addition to malted barley. Honey character should be present in aroma and flavor but should not be overpowering.
aroma: Malt and hop aroma vary depending on intention of brewer. Honey character should be present but not overpowering.
appearance: Color is very light to black depending on underlying style. Clear to hazy is acceptable.
flavor: Honey character present but not overpowering. Malt and hop flavor vary depending on intention of brewer. Perceived bitterness very low to very high.
comments: Honey Beers may be brewed to a traditional style or may be experimental. IPA and Imperial IPA brewed with honey are categorized as Experimental India Pale Ale.
characteristicingredients: Honey as a fermentable sugar in addition to malted barley.

name: Rye Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Any beer with sufficient rye in the grist that rye character is evident. Rye is often described as spicy, black pepper-like, or earthy.
aroma: Low levels of spicy and fruity ester aromas are typical. Yeast-derived aromas (clove-like or other phenolics) when consistent with underlying beer style. In darker versions, malt aromas may include low cocoa/chocolate, caramel, aromatic toffee, or biscuit. Hop aroma low to medium-high. Diacetyl should not be present. Low to medium yeast aroma may be present in versions packaged with yeast.
appearance: Color is straw to copper in lighter versions; dark amber to dark brown in darker versions. Chill haze acceptable in versions without yeast; versions with yeast may be hazy to very cloudy.
flavor: Spicy, black pepper-like, or earthy rye character. Malt varies with underlying style. Hop flavor low to medium-high. Perceived bitterness low to medium. Low level roastiness or graininess acceptable in darker versions.
mouthfeel: Body is low to medium. Rye can impart textural qualities ranging from dry and crisp to smooth and velvety.
comments: Beers brewed with rye that do not exhibit rye character are categorized in other beer styles. Versions served with yeast should possess a full yeasty mouthfeel.
characteristicingredients: Sufficient rye for rye character to be evident; ale or lager yeast.

name: Ginjo Beer or Sake-Yeast Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: A beer brewed with sake yeast or sake (koji) enzymes, with mild fruitiness, earthiness, and umami/mushroom-like protein attributes characteristic of sake.
aroma: Sake-like character with mild fruitiness and mild earthiness expressed as mushroom or umami protein-like attributes. Very low to medium malt aroma. Hop aroma low to medium and in harmony with sake-like character. A high amount of alcohol may be evident.
appearance: Color is pale to dark brown. Slight chill haze is acceptable.
flavor: Sake-like fruitiness, earthiness, mushroom/umami. Malt very low to medium. Hop flavor low to medium in harmony with sake-like character. Perceived bitterness low to medium.
mouthfeel: Body varies depending on original gravity. Mouthfeel also varies. High carbonation should be present.
comments: Brewed with sake yeast or sake (koji) enzymes; unique byproducts should be distinctive and in harmony with other elements.
characteristicingredients: Sake yeast or sake (koji) enzymes.

name: Fresh Hop Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Ales or lagers brewed with freshly harvested hops (typically within 72 hours of harvest). Prominent fresh hop aroma and flavor including green grass-like, fresh mown hay/grass character.
aroma: Fresh hop aroma prominent, exhibiting green grass-like, fresh mown hay/grass or other fresh hop attributes. Malt aroma varies with underlying style. Fruity esters may be present at levels consistent with the underlying beer style.
appearance: Color varies with underlying style. Chill haze is acceptable at low temperatures. Hop haze is allowable at any temperature.
flavor: Fresh hop flavor prominent with green grass-like, fresh mown hay/grass attributes. Malt varies with underlying style. Perceived bitterness varies with underlying style.
mouthfeel: Body varies with underlying style. Should exhibit some perceptible chlorophyllic character, mouthfeel, and/or texture harmonious with base style.
comments: Brewed with freshly harvested hops, typically no more than 72 hours prior to being added. Hops may be undried fresh or frozen cones or ground material, or freshly kilned dried cones or pellets. Typically consumed while fresh. Fresh Hop India Pale Ales are classified here rather than as Experimental IPAs.
characteristicingredients: Freshly harvested hops (fresh, frozen, or freshly kilned).

name: Aged Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Any beer aged for over one year in non-wooden vessels. Aged character should be expressed in mouthfeel, aroma, and flavor, often as sherry-like and fruity flavors with balanced overall character.
aroma: Sherry-like and fruity flavors often develop during aging. Hop character often changes. Malt and hop aroma vary with underlying style. Aged character may be the result of oxidative reactions that bring flavor components into harmony or create unique flavors.
appearance: Varies with underlying style.
flavor: Aged character expressed in flavor. Overall character should be balanced and without aggressive flavors. Sherry-like and fruity. Malt and hop flavor vary with underlying style.
comments: Beers with high hopping rates, roast malt, high alcohol content, and complex herbal/smoke/fruit character are the best candidates for aging. May be aged in bottles, cans, kegs or other non-wooden vessels. Mildly-flavored beers are more likely to develop aggressive and unpleasant oxidation. Brett Beers, Sour Beers or any beers exhibiting attributes of aging with microflora must be categorized elsewhere.
characteristicingredients: Underlying beer style with extended aging time (>1 year).

name: Other Strong Ale or Lager
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Beers of any style intentionally brewed to higher alcohol content than defined within that style's guidelines. Balance is achieved between the style's characteristics and the additional alcohol.
aroma: Varies with underlying style.
appearance: Varies with underlying style.
flavor: Varies with underlying style. Should achieve a balance between the style's characteristics and the additional alcohol.
comments: Entrants should review all other Strong/Imperial categories (Strong Porter, Imperial Red, Belgian Strong, etc.) first before entering this category. IPA entries that fall outside Imperial IPA specifications and/or have added ingredients such as fruit or spices should be entered as Experimental IPA. Wood- and barrel-aged versions are categorized elsewhere.
characteristicingredients: Varies with underlying style.

name: Gluten-Free Beer
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Lagers, ales, or other beers made from gluten-free fermentable sugars, grains, and converted carbohydrates. All ingredients must be free of gluten.
aroma: Varies with underlying style. Grains and fermentables which differ from typical beer ingredients will produce flavor and aroma outcomes that differ from traditional versions.
appearance: Varies with underlying style.
flavor: Varies with underlying style. Differences from traditional versions are expected and acceptable.
comments: Beers brewed with barley, wheat, spelt, rye, and other gluten-containing ingredients may not be categorized as Gluten-Free. Gluten-Free Beers may contain malted grains that are gluten-free. Gluten-reduced beers (where gluten has been reduced by enzymes or other processes) should be categorized in the classic style category most appropriate for the beer, rather than as Gluten-Free Beer.
characteristicingredients: Gluten-free grains and fermentables; all ingredients must be free of gluten.

name: Non-Alcohol Malt Beverage
category: All Origin Hybrid/Mixed Lagers or Ales
overallimpression: Non-alcohol (N/A) malt beverages emulating the character of any beer style defined within the guidelines but with no or nearly no alcohol (less than 0.5 percent abv).
aroma: Varies with underlying style. Will have a profile lacking the complexity and balance of flavors that beers containing alcohol display.
appearance: Varies with underlying style.
flavor: Varies with underlying style. Should not be given negative evaluations for reasons related to the absence of alcohol.
comments: Brewers will be asked to verify that the alcohol content of entries are <0.5% abv.
characteristicingredients: Underlying beer style ingredients with alcohol removal/limitation processes.
